---
title: "Zapped out of existence! Home-field advantage decomposition patterns persist after removal of leaf-associated microbes"
author: "CJ Spiegel et al. 2023, in prep"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 4
    toc_float: yes
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: '4'
  editor_options:
      chunk_output_type: console
---

### Project overview
For Project ‘RAD’ Alder, gamma irradiated (phyllosphere present (P)) vs. non-irradiated (phyllosphere absent (AB)) leaf packs deployed at “home” or “away” sites tests the role of phyllosphere microbial communities on home-field advantage patterns in leaf litter decomposition. We utilized a reciprocal transplant experiment between the Hoko and Sekiu rivers on the Olympic Peninsula under four treatment conditions: phyllosphere present-home (PH), phyllosphere present-away (PAW), phyllosphere absent-home (ABH), phyllosphere absent-away (ABAW) to ask the following questions: 

1) How does dispersal from the regional species pool (i.e., source communities) influence community composition on the leaf packs?
2) How does microbial community composition (alpha and beta diversity) and function differ between the leaf packs, as well as change over the course of decomposition?
3) How does alder stoichiometric and secondary chemical composition differ between the leaf packs, as well as change over the course of decomposition?
4) How does microbial metabolization differ between the leaf packs, as well as change over the course of decomposition?
5) How does particulate leaf mass loss differ between the leaf packs? 


**DATA SETS**  
This data set focuses on:  

(1) **Metabolism assay** 
(2) **Leaf mass loss** using % mass loss and HFAI
(3) **Alder C and N isotopic composition**
(4) **Alder secondary chemistry (via LC-MS/MS)** 
```{r setup chunk, setup, include = FALSE, cache=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!require('knitr')) install.packages('knitr'); library('knitr')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.align='center')

# load packages
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman") # for rapid install if not in library

# use pacman to load all the packages you are missing!
pacman::p_load('knitr', 'lme4', 'lmerTest', 'tidyverse', 'magrittr', 'effects', 'plyr', 'dplyr', 
               'plotrix', 'car',"gridExtra", "cowplot", "tools", "mgcv", "gratia", "tidymv", "ggthemes", "ggpubr", "scales", "visreg", "mgcViz")

Fig.formatting<-(theme_classic()) +
  theme(text=element_text(size=25),
        legend.text.align = 0,
        panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA, colour = "black", size=1),
        aspect.ratio=1, 
        axis.ticks.length=unit(0.25, "cm"),
        axis.text.y=element_text(
        margin=unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5), "cm"), colour="black", size=20), 
        axis.text.x=element_text(
        margin=unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5), "cm"), colour="black", size=20)) +
  theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"), legend.position = "right") +
  theme(aspect.ratio=0.5) 




```

**Note on absorbance**
Iodonitrotetrazolium chloride (INT) is utilized as a redox dye to gauge microbial activity in assays. Microbes with metabolic activity can reduce INT to a formazan compound, causing a color change from yellow (oxidized form) to red or purple (reduced form). This reduction reaction is often catalyzed by dehydrogenase enzymes present in metabolically active cells. The formazan product is  insoluble and has a distinct color (i.e., absorbance), leading to a visible change in the solution's color. The intensity of the color or absorbance is indicative of microbial metabolic activity.

We measured absorbance at 450 nm (see Blenkinsopp & Lock, 1990; Friedel et al., 1994; Jackson et al., 2013; Bhupathiraju et al., 1998). Time course measurements, or absorbance readings at different intervals (e.g., 30 mins, 2 hrs, 16 hrs, 24 hrs, 48 hrs, and 72 hrs), allowed for tracking changes in microbial metabolic activity over time.

### Import metabolism assay data
```{r}
#add data files
meta.day1.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_1_450_clean.csv")
meta.day1.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_1_590_clean.csv")


meta.day3.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_3_450_clean.csv")
meta.day3.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_3_590_clean.csv")


meta.day5.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_5_450_clean.csv")
meta.day5.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_5_590_clean.csv")

meta.day7.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_7_450_clean.csv")
meta.day7.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_7_590_clean.csv")

meta.day11.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_11_450_clean.csv")
meta.day11.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_11_590_clean.csv")

meta.day17.450.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_17_450_clean.csv")
meta.day17.590.df=read.csv("/Users/Cody1/Desktop/GitHub/RAD Alder/data/assay/day_17_590_clean.csv")


#merge 


meta.450.df= rbind(
  meta.day1.450.df, 
  meta.day3.450.df,
  meta.day5.450.df,
  meta.day7.450.df, 
  meta.day11.450.df, 
  meta.day17.450.df
  )

meta.590.df=rbind(
  meta.day1.590.df,
  meta.day3.590.df,
  meta.day5.590.df,
  meta.day7.590.df,
  meta.day11.590.df, 
  meta.day17.590.df
)

#remove well_id columns
meta.450.df <- meta.450.df[, !colnames(meta.450.df) %in% "well_id"]
meta.590.df <- meta.590.df[, !colnames(meta.590.df) %in% "well_id"]


#create groups based on deployment and metabolite pair

#if deploy is HOK, metabolite is HOK (home)
#if deplpy is SEK, metaboltie is SEK (home)
#if deploy is HOK, metaboltie is SEK (away)
#if deploy is SEK, metabolite is HOK (away)

meta.450.df$group <- 
  ifelse(
  meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$metabolite == "HOK", "H",
   ifelse(
  meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$metabolite == "SEK", "H",
   ifelse(
   meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$metabolite == "SEK", "AW",
   ifelse(
   meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$metabolite == "HOK", "AW", NA  # Default value if none of the conditions are met
                )
              )
            )
          )

meta.590.df$group <- 
  ifelse(
  meta.590.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.590.df$metabolite == "HOK", "H",
   ifelse(
  meta.590.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.590.df$metabolite == "SEK", "H",
   ifelse(
   meta.590.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.590.df$metabolite == "SEK", "AW",
   ifelse(
   meta.590.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.590.df$metabolite == "HOK", "AW", NA  # Default value if none of the conditions are met
                )
              )
            )
          )


#add hours rather than minutes
meta.450.df$hours <- 
  ifelse(
  meta.450.df$time == "30", "0.5",
   ifelse(
  meta.450.df$time == "120", "2",
   ifelse(
   meta.450.df$time == "960", "16",
   ifelse(
   meta.450.df$time == "1440", "24",
    ifelse(
   meta.450.df$time == "2880", "48",
   ifelse(
   meta.450.df$time == "4320", "72", NA  # Default value if none of the conditions are met
                )
              )
            )
          )
)
)

meta.590.df$hours <- 
  ifelse(
  meta.590.df$time == "30", "0.5",
   ifelse(
  meta.590.df$time == "120", "2",
   ifelse(
   meta.590.df$time == "960", "16",
   ifelse(
   meta.590.df$time == "1440", "24",
    ifelse(
   meta.590.df$time == "2880", "48",
   ifelse(
   meta.590.df$time == "4320", "72", NA  # Default value if none of the conditions are met
                )
              )
            )
          )
)
)

#reorder hours
meta.450.df <- meta.450.df %>% mutate(hours = factor(hours, levels = c("0.5", "2", "16", "24", "48", "72")))
meta.590.df <- meta.590.df %>% mutate(hours = factor(hours, levels = c("0.5", "2", "16", "24", "48", "72")))

#make colummn factors
meta.450.df$treatment=as.factor(meta.450.df$treatment)
meta.450.df$tree_id=as.factor(meta.450.df$tree_id)
meta.450.df$deploy=as.factor(meta.450.df$deploy)
meta.450.df$metabolite=as.factor(meta.450.df$metabolite)
meta.450.df$group=as.factor(meta.450.df$group)
meta.450.df$hours=factor(meta.450.df$hours)

meta.590.df$treatment=as.factor(meta.590.df$treatment)
meta.590.df$tree_id=as.factor(meta.590.df$tree_id)
meta.590.df$deploy=as.factor(meta.590.df$deploy)
meta.590.df$metabolite=as.factor(meta.590.df$metabolite)
meta.590.df$group=as.factor(meta.590.df$group)
meta.590.df$hours=factor(meta.590.df$hours)

#make pairing for 4 treatments
meta.450.df <- meta.450.df %>%
  mutate(pairing = paste(treatment, group, sep = "-"))

meta.450.df <- meta.450.df %>% 
  mutate(pairing = factor(pairing, levels = c("P-AW", "P-H", "AB-AW", "AB-H")))

#create df
meta.450.df <- meta.450.df %>%
  mutate(
    new.trt = case_when(
      treatment == "P" ~ "LAM+FLM",
      treatment == "AB" ~ "FLM-only"))

#subset by true home 
deploy.hok_only.df=meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy=="HOK" & meta.450.df$tree_id=="HOK",]
deploy.sek_only.df=meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy=="SEK" & meta.450.df$tree_id=="SEK",]
#merge
true_home.meta.df=rbind(deploy.hok_only.df, deploy.sek_only.df)

#subset by false home 
deploy.hok.df=meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy=="HOK" & meta.450.df$tree_id=="SEK",]
deploy.sek.df=meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy=="SEK" & meta.450.df$tree_id=="HOK",]
#merge
false_home.meta.df=rbind(deploy.hok.df, deploy.sek.df)


#create subsets
LAM_FL.df=true_home.meta.df%>%
  filter(new.trt=="LAM+FL")%>%
  select(tree_id, deploy, metabolite,tree_num, site_num, group, decomp_day, od_corrected_b_n, new.trt)%>%
  arrange(decomp_day)

FL_only.df=true_home.meta.df%>%
   filter(new.trt=="FLM-only")%>%
  select(tree_id, deploy, metabolite,tree_num, site_num, group, decomp_day, od_corrected_b_n, new.trt)%>%
arrange(decomp_day)
```

#### determine which absorbance wavelength to use. Various studies (as those cited above) measured the absorbance of INT at both 450 and 590 nm. This provides some flexibility for us to determine exactly which to use based on the conditions present in our system (i.e., rivers, within the wells, external conditions of the lab space, etc). Factors such as these can influence the rate of metabolization, and thus the OD values from the absorbance reader. Additionally, determining which reading time (30 mins, 2 hrs, 16 hrs, etc.) at which the OD values were measured is important. Past work has measured absorbance at 30 mins, while other recommend 2-16 hrs. However, given the specific conditions of our field experiment, it has been suggested that 24 hrs may be required for an accurate metabolic reading. Additionally, we need to include a hierarchical modeling framework to capture the nonlinear trends in the data (a priori parabolic, or unimodal, metabolization curves across decomposition day) and repeated measures for each leaf pack. 

Let's first visualize the raw OD readings at both 450 and 590 nm.
```{r}

#plot at 450 nm
meta.450.df %>%
    filter(time == "1440")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_point()+
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "standardized optical density (z score)")+
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
   guides(linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")))+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )


# plot at 590 nm
meta.df %>%
    filter(nm == "590", time == "1440")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = interaction(group, treatment), linetype = treatment, color = group, fill = group), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k = 6), alpha = 0.1) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "standardized optical density (z score)")+
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
   guides(linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")))+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )

```

Based on the literature, we will use an absorbance wavelength of 450 nm which is appropriate for the metabolic indicator (INT) in the assay. 
### Use 450 nm absorbance readings
```{r}
#by group and treatment using loess
meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.numeric(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = treatment, fill = treatment, linetype = group, group = interaction(group, treatment)), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, alpha = 0.1) +
    geom_line(aes(y = neg), color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = neg - sd(neg), ymax = neg + sd(neg)), fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 5, 9, 13, 17))+
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
guides(
    linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")),
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),  # Set order for the color legend
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1)   # Set order for the fill legend
  ) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )

#by group and treatment using gam
meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "16")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = treatment, fill = treatment, linetype = group, group = interaction(group, treatment)), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k =4), alpha = 0.1) +
    geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), method = "gam", color = "red", fill= "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)")+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 5, 9, 13, 17))+
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
guides(
    linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")),
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),  # Set order for the color legend
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1)   # Set order for the fill legend
  ) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )

meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = od_corrected_b_n, group = interaction (group, treatment, tree_num)))+
  geom_smooth(aes(color = treatment, fill = treatment, linetype = group, group = interaction(group, treatment)), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k=4), alpha = 0.1) +
      geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, k=4), color = alpha("red", 0.5), fill= alpha("red", 0.5), linetype = "longdash", size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)")+
scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
guides(
    linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")),
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),  # Set order for the color legend
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1)   # Set order for the fill legend
  ) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )


#####################

meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, group = interaction(group, treatment), color = treatment, fill = treatment)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", aes(shape = treatment), show.legend = F, size = 2.3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = treatment,fill = treatment, linetype=group), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k = 6), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_point(aes(y= neg), color = alpha("red", 0.3), shape = 15)+
  geom_line(aes(y = neg), color = alpha("red", 0.3), linetype = "longdash", size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)") +
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
  guides(
    linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")),
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),  # Set order for the color legend
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1)   # Set order for the fill legend
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )


```


These are the final plots
```{r}
#####################
meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, group = interaction(group, treatment), color = treatment, fill = treatment)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", aes(shape = treatment), show.legend = T, size = 2.3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = treatment,fill = treatment, linetype=group), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k = 6), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_point(aes(y= neg,  shape = "neg"), color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F)+
  geom_line(aes(y = neg), color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F, linetype = "longdash", size = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)") +
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
  guides(
    linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")),
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),  # Set order for the color legend
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1)   # Set order for the fill legend
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )+
  Fig.formatting

assay.plot

ggsave("figures/assay.plot.pdf", assay.plot, width = 17, height = 8)


#4 treatment plot
trt.assay.plot=meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, color = pairing, group = pairing)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", show.legend = F, size = 2.3) +
    geom_point(aes(y= neg,  shape = "neg"), size= 2.3, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F)+
  geom_smooth(aes(linetype= pairing, fill = pairing),method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k = 6), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), fill = alpha ("red", 0.3), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, span= 0.6, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F, linetype = "longdash") +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "absorbance (OD450 nm)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,3))+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
    scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +  # Adjust the expand argument
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )+
  Fig.formatting

trt.assay.plot

ggsave("figures/trt.assay.plot.pdf", trt.assay.plot, width = 15, height = 8)

meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, color = treatment, fill = treatment, group = interaction(group, treatment))) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", show.legend = F, size = 2.3) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", show.legend = F, size = 1) +

    geom_point(aes(y= neg,  shape = "neg"), size= 2.3, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F)+
  geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), fill = alpha ("red", 0.3), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, span= 0.6, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F, linetype = "longdash") +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "absorbance (OD450 nm)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,3))+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
    scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +  # Adjust the expand argument
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )+
  Fig.formatting


meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, color = pairing, group = pairing)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", show.legend = F, size = 2.3) +
    geom_point(aes(y= neg,  shape = "neg"), size= 2.3, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F)+
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", show.legend = F, size = 0.75) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), fill = alpha ("red", 0.3), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, span= 0.6, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = F, linetype = "longdash") +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "absorbance (OD450 nm)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,3))+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
    scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +  # Adjust the expand argument
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )+
  Fig.formatting


meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24") %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n, color = pairing, group = pairing)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", show.legend = FALSE, size = 2.3) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.1, show.legend = FALSE) +  # Add error bars
  geom_point(aes(y = neg, shape = "neg"), size = 2.3, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = FALSE) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", show.legend = FALSE, size = 0.75) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = neg), fill = alpha("red", 0.3), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, span = 0.6, color = alpha("red", 0.3), show.legend = FALSE, linetype = "longdash") +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "absorbance (OD450 nm)") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 3)) +
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)
  ) +
  Fig.formatting


```


```{r}
#This pattern suggests that microbial communities from the intermediate stages of decomposition may be particularly well-equipped or adapted to metabolize the plant metabolites. 

#The hypothesis about a unimodal trend in abundance and diversity, where early and low decomposition days show low abundance and diversity, and intermediate decomposition days show high abundance and diversity, is a reasonable expectation. It aligns with the concept of priority effects, where early colonizers influence substrate conditions and, in some cases, facilitate the establishment of subsequent colonizers.

#However, the findings from the metabolization assay contradict this hypothesis. The observation that early-stage days exhibit lower metabolization despite the potential presence of key enzymes in those microbes challenges the initial hypothesis.

#This discrepancy suggests that the relationship between microbial community composition, their functional attributes (such as enzyme production), and the metabolization of plant extracts is more complex than initially anticipated. It's possible that other factors, such as the specific secondary metabolites present in the plant extracts, microbial interactions, or ecological conditions, influence the metabolic activity of the communities.


#by 4 treatments
meta.450.df %>%
  filter(hours == "24")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = decomp_day, y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(linetype= pairing, color = pairing, fill = pairing), method = 'gam', formula = y ~ s(x, k = 4), alpha = 0.1) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)")+
    guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Treatment"), fill= guide_legend(title = "Treatment"), linetype = guide_legend(title = "Treatment"))+
   scale_linetype_manual(name = "Treatment",
                        values = c("P-AW" = "solid", "P-H" = "solid", "AB-AW" = "dotdash", "AB-H" = "dotdash")) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(title = "Treatment"), fill = guide_legend(title = "Treatment"), linetype = guide_legend(title = "Treatment"))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 5, 9, 13, 17))+
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )


#HFAI shows P packs to have greater incidence of HFA, while AB show greater magnitude
meta.450.df %>%
   filter(hours == "24", group == "H")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.numeric(decomp_day), y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_smooth(aes( color = treatment, fill = treatment), method = 'loess', formula = y ~x, alpha = 0.1) +
  labs(x = "decomposition day", y = "optical density (450 nm)")+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 5, 9, 13, 17))+
  scale_color_colorblind() +
  scale_fill_colorblind() +
   guides(linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "cornflowerblue")))+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),      # Adjust text size for axis labels
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),     # Adjust text size for axis titles
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Adjust text size for legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),   # Adjust text size for legend title
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16)      # Adjust text size for facet labels
  )

#In the context of the Homefield Advantage Index (HFAI), "incidence" and "magnitude" can be understood as follows:

#Incidence of HFAI:

#Definition: Incidence refers to the frequency or occurrence of HFAI events. In the Context of HFAI:  the incidence of HFAI is more in the "present" treatment, it means that there are more instances or occurrences where the HFAI is observed when the phyllosphere community is present. This could be interpreted as a higher frequency of home advantage events in terms of decomposition when the community is present.

#Magnitude of HFAI: Definition: Magnitude refers to the size, extent, or intensity of the HFAI events.  the magnitude is greater in the "absent" treatment, it suggests that, although the frequency might be lower, when HFAI events occur in the absence of the phyllosphere community, they have a larger impact or are more pronounced in terms of decomposition. The decomposition advantage at home (Hoko) is more significant when the community is absent, leading to a higher magnitude of the HFAI.

#In summary, the "incidence" reflects how often HFAI events occur, while the "magnitude" indicates the strength or impact of those events when they do occur. Both aspects are important for understanding the dynamics of the Homefield Advantage Index in your experimental conditions.

#Microbial communities metabolizing their home metabolite exhibit higher activity in the presence of the phyllosphere community. This is consistent with the HFai framework, where the relative advantage of home metabolite metabolization is observed in the ecological context of the phyllosphere community being present.



```


#### z score optical density
##### Day 1
```{r}
##### Day 1
#HOK 1 subset
d1.hok1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1.hok1_meta.df=d1.hok1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#HOK 2 subset
d1.hok2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1.hok2_meta.df=d1.hok2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 1 subset
d1.sek1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1.sek1_meta.df=d1.sek1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 2 subset
d1.sek2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1.sek2_meta.df=d1.sek2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#merge z scored dfs
d1.z_scored_df = rbind(d1.hok1_meta.df, d1.hok2_meta.df, d1.sek1_meta.df, d1.sek2_meta.df)

#anovas
aov_d1=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= d1.z_scored_df )

aov_d1_od=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing, data= d1.z_scored_df )

aov_d1_od_trt=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group, data= d1.z_scored_df )


AIC(aov_d1, aov_d1_od,aov_d1_od_trt)
summary(aov_d1_od)
plot(aov_d1_od)

d1.z_scored_df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= pairing, y =od_corrected_b_n ))+
    geom_boxplot()+
      geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") 


############################
```


This is now looking at a "true" home scenario for the comparison between sampled communities against 'home' or 'away' metabolites. In this instance, leaves are sourced from Hoko and deployed at Hoko, or sourced from Sekiu and deployed at  Sekiu. The sampled communities are then "home" communities found at their respective sites with either leaf-associated microbes (LAMs) and free-living microbes (FLMs), FLM-only, or LAM-only. These three groups allow us to see how phyllosphere microbes (i.e., LAMS) influence metabolization of home or away metabolites.

TRUE HOME (HOK to HOK or SEK to SEK)
z scored by deployment site and decomposition day
```{r}
library(multcompView)
#filter by correct hours
true_home.meta.df=true_home.meta.df%>%
  filter(hours =="24")

#z score
##### Day 1
#HOK 1 subset
d1_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_hok1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_hok1.true_home.df=d1_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_hok1.true_home.df=d3_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_hok1.true_home.df=d5_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_hok1.true_home.df=d7_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_hok1.true_home.df=d11_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_hok1.true_home.df=d17_hok1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#HOK 2 subset
d1_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_hok2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "HOK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_hok2.true_home.df=d1_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_hok2.true_home.df=d3_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_hok2.true_home.df=d5_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_hok2.true_home.df=d7_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_hok2.true_home.df=d11_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_hok2.true_home.df=d17_hok2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#merge all hok
all.hok.true_home.df=rbind(d1_hok1.true_home.df,
                           d3_hok1.true_home.df,
                           d5_hok1.true_home.df,
                           d7_hok1.true_home.df,
                           d11_hok1.true_home.df,
                           d17_hok1.true_home.df,
                           
                          d1_hok2.true_home.df,
                           d3_hok2.true_home.df,
                           d5_hok2.true_home.df,
                           d7_hok2.true_home.df,
                           d11_hok2.true_home.df,
                           d17_hok2.true_home.df)

#SEK 1 subset
d1_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_sek1.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "1" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_sek1.true_home.df=d1_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_sek1.true_home.df=d3_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_sek1.true_home.df=d5_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_sek1.true_home.df=d7_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_sek1.true_home.df=d11_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_sek1.true_home.df=d17_sek1.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#SEK 2 subset
d1_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_sek2.true_home.df <- data.frame(true_home.meta.df[true_home.meta.df$deploy == "SEK" & true_home.meta.df$site_num == "2" & true_home.meta.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_sek2.true_home.df=d1_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_sek2.true_home.df=d3_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_sek2.true_home.df=d5_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_sek2.true_home.df=d7_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_sek2.true_home.df=d11_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_sek2.true_home.df=d17_sek2.true_home.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#merge all sek
all.sek.true_home.df=rbind(d1_sek1.true_home.df,
                           d3_sek1.true_home.df,
                           d5_sek1.true_home.df,
                           d7_sek1.true_home.df,
                           d11_sek1.true_home.df,
                           d17_sek1.true_home.df,
                           
                          d1_sek2.true_home.df,
                           d3_sek2.true_home.df,
                           d5_sek2.true_home.df,
                           d7_sek2.true_home.df,
                           d11_sek2.true_home.df,
                           d17_sek2.true_home.df)

#merge all z scored dfs
merged.true_home_z.df = rbind(all.hok.true_home.df, all.sek.true_home.df)
merged.true_home_z.df <- merged.true_home_z.df %>% 
  mutate(pairing = factor(pairing, levels = c("P-H", "P-AW", "AB-H", "AB-AW")))

# Calculate means for each decomp_day
#od readings
od_means_per_day <- merged.true_home_z.df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(decomp_day) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_od = mean(od_corrected_b_n, na.rm = TRUE))

od_true.home.boxplot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= treatment, y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = group))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
  xlab("treatment")+
  ylab(expression("optical density (OD"[450~nm]*")"))+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day, labeller = labeller(decomp_day = c("1" = "Day 1", "3" = "Day 3", "5" = "Day 5", "7" = "Day 7", "11" = "Day 11", "17" = "Day 17"))) +
  ggtitle('"True home" comparisons')+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
           geom_hline(data=od_means_per_day, aes(yintercept = mean_od, linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
      theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave("figures/od_true.home.boxplot.pdf", od_true.home.boxplot, width = 8, height = 6)

##### models by decomp day

#Day 1
d1_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "1", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d1_aov)
d1_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d1_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d1_cld <- multcompLetters4(d1_aov, d1_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d1_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "1")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d1_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d1_cld$`treatment:group`)
d1_meta.table$cld <- d1_cld$Letters
#plot
d1_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day =="1")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("")+
  ylab(expression("optical density (OD"[450~nm]*")"))+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 1")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d1_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-2, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting


#Day 3
d3_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "3", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d3_aov)
d3_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d3_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d3_cld <- multcompLetters4(d3_aov, d3_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d3_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "3")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d3_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d3_cld$`treatment:group`)
d3_meta.table$cld <- d3_cld$Letters
#plot
d3_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day =="3")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("")+
  ylab("")+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 3")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d3_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-2, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting

#Day 5
d5_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "5", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d5_aov)
d5_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d5_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d5_cld <- multcompLetters4(d5_aov, d5_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d5_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "5")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d5_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d5_cld$`treatment:group`)
d5_meta.table$cld <- d5_cld$Letters
#plot
d5_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "5")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("")+
  ylab("")+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 5")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d5_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-2, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting

d5_meta.plot

#Day 7
d7_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "7", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d7_aov)
d7_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d7_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d7_cld <- multcompLetters4(d7_aov, d7_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d7_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "7")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d7_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d7_cld$`treatment:group`)
d7_meta.table$cld <- d7_cld$Letters
#plot
d7_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day =="7")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("treatment")+
  ylab("")+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 7")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d7_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-3, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting

d7_meta.plot

#Day 11
d11_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "11", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d11_aov)
d11_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d11_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d11_cld <- multcompLetters4(d11_aov, d11_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d11_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "11")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d11_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d11_cld$`treatment:group`)
d11_meta.table$cld <- d11_cld$Letters
#plot
d11_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day =="11")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("")+
  ylab("")+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 11")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d11_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-2, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting

d11_meta.plot


#Day 17
d17_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group, subset = decomp_day == "17", data = na.omit(merged.true_home_z.df))
summary(d17_aov)
d17_meta_tHSD=TukeyHSD(d17_aov, ordered = F, conf.level = 0.95)
d17_cld <- multcompLetters4(d17_aov, d17_meta_tHSD)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
d17_meta.table <- merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day == "17")%>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean=mean(od_corrected_b_n), quant = quantile(od_corrected_b_n, probs = 0.75)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean))

# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
d17_cld <- as.data.frame.list(d17_cld$`treatment:group`)
d17_meta.table$cld <- d17_cld$Letters
#plot
d17_meta.plot=merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  filter(decomp_day=="17")%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-"), y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = interaction(treatment, group, sep ="-")))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,4))+
  xlab("")+
  ylab("")+
  labs(fill = "hfa")+
  ggtitle("Day 17")+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  geom_text(data = d17_meta.table, aes(y = quant, label = cld), color = "blue", fontface= "bold",size = 5, vjust=-2, hjust =-0.76)+
  Fig.formatting


all.days_meta.plot=plot_grid(
  d1_meta.plot + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  d3_meta.plot + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  d5_meta.plot + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  d7_meta.plot + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  d11_meta.plot+ theme(legend.position = "none"),
  d17_meta.plot + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  rel_widths = c(8,8,8,8,8,8), nrow=2)

ggsave("figures/all.days_meta.plot.pdf", all.days_meta.plot, width = 22, height = 10)



#z score
z_means_per_day <- merged.true_home_z.df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(decomp_day) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_z = mean(z_score, na.rm = TRUE))

library(ggthemes)
merged.true_home_z.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  filter(group == "H")%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= treatment, y = z_score, fill = treatment))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4)+
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day)+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(data=z_means_per_day, aes(yintercept = mean_z, linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()

#false home
means_per_day <- false_home.meta.df %>%
  group_by(decomp_day) %>%
  summarise(mean_z = mean(z_score, na.rm = TRUE))

false_home.meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  filter(hours =="24")%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= new.trt, y = od_corrected_b_n, fill = group))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
           geom_hline(data= means_per_day, aes(yintercept = mean_od, linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()

#find difference between LAM+FL vs FL only
lam_only.df=all.true_home.df%>%
  dplyr::group_by(tree_id,  deploy, metabolite,tree_num, site_num, group, decomp_day)%>%
  dplyr::summarize(od_corrected_b_n = sum(od_corrected_b_n[new.trt == "LAM+FL"]) - sum(od_corrected_b_n[new.trt == "FLM-only"]))%>%
  mutate(new.trt="LAM-only")%>%
  arrange(decomp_day)



AIC(d1_aov, d1_aov2)

summary(aov2)
plot(aov2)

hist(resid(aov3))

```

This is raw and z scored of all communities
```{r}
meta.450.df=meta.450.df%>%
  filter(hours =="24")
#z score
##### Day 1
#HOK 1 subset
d1_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_hok1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_hok1.df=d1_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_hok1.df=d3_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_hok1.df=d5_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_hok1.df=d7_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_hok1.df=d11_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_hok1.df=d17_hok1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#HOK 2 subset
d1_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_hok2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_hok2.df=d1_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_hok2.df=d3_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_hok2.df=d5_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_hok2.df=d7_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_hok2.df=d11_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_hok2.df=d17_hok2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#merge all hok
all.hok.df=rbind(d1_hok1.df,
                           d3_hok1.df,
                           d5_hok1.df,
                           d7_hok1.df,
                           d11_hok1.df,
                           d17_hok1.df,
                           
                          d1_hok2.df,
                           d3_hok2.df,
                           d5_hok2.df,
                           d7_hok2.df,
                           d11_hok2.df,
                           d17_hok2.df)

#SEK 1 subset
d1_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_sek1.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "1" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_sek1.df=d1_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_sek1.df=d3_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_sek1.df=d5_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_sek1.df=d7_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_sek1.df=d11_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_sek1.df=d17_sek1.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#SEK 2 subset
d1_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "1", ])
d3_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3", ])
d5_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5", ])
d7_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "7", ])
d11_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "11", ])
d17_sek2.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" & meta.450.df$site_num == "2" & meta.450.df$decomp_day == "17", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d1_sek2.df=d1_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d3_sek2.df=d3_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d5_sek2.df=d5_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d7_sek2.df=d7_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d11_sek2.df=d11_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))
d17_sek2.df=d17_sek2.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#merge all hok
all.sek.df=rbind(d1_sek1.df,
                           d3_sek1.df,
                           d5_sek1.df,
                           d7_sek1.df,
                           d11_sek1.df,
                           d17_sek1.df,
                           
                          d1_sek2.df,
                           d3_sek2.df,
                           d5_sek2.df,
                           d7_sek2.df,
                           d11_sek2.df,
                           d17_sek2.df)

#merge z scored dfs
merged_z.df = rbind(all.hok.df, all.sek.df)

 


#all combinations with meta.df
means_per_day <- meta.450.df %>%
  group_by(decomp_day) %>%
  summarise(mean_od = mean(od_corrected_b_n, na.rm = TRUE))

meta.450.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= treatment, y = od_corrected_b_n, color = group))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  geom_hline(data= means_per_day, aes(yintercept = mean_od, linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()

d1_all.meta_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n~new.trt*group, subset = decomp_day == "1", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

d1_all.meta_lmer=lmer(od_corrected_b_n~new.trt*group + (1|deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1| tree_num) + (1 | tree_id), subset = decomp_day == "1", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

d3_all.meta_lmer=lmer(od_corrected_b_n~new.trt*group + (1|deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1| tree_num) + (1 | tree_id), subset = decomp_day == "3", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))


plot(resid(d1_all.meta_lmer))
summary(d3_all.meta_lmer)

AIC(d3_all.meta_aov, d3_all.meta_lmer)

d3_all.meta_aov=aov(od_corrected_b_n~new.trt*group, subset = decomp_day == "3", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))
TukeyHSD(d3_all.meta_aov)
summary(d3_all.meta_aov)

hist(resid(d3_all.meta_aov))
plot(resid(d3_all.meta_aov))
# z score
means_per_day <- merged_z.df %>%
  group_by(decomp_day) %>%
  summarise(mean_z = mean(z_score, na.rm = TRUE))


merged_z.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  filter(hours =="24")%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= new.trt, y = z_score, color = group))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
           geom_hline(data= means_per_day, aes(yintercept = mean_z, linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic()

d1_all.meta_z_aov=aov(z_score~new.trt*group, subset = decomp_day == "1", data = na.omit(merged_z.df))

d3_all.meta_z_aov=aov(z_score~new.trt*group, subset = decomp_day == "3", data = na.omit(merged_z.df))
hist(resid(d3_all.meta_z_aov))

plot(resid(d3_all.meta_z_aov))

summary(all.meta_z_aov)

AIC(d3_all.meta_aov, d3_all.meta_z_aov)

plot(resid(all.meta_z_aov))
plot(all.meta_aov)


```



##### Day 3
```{r}
##### Day 1
#HOK 1 subset
d3.hok1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d3.hok1_meta.df=d3.hok1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#HOK 2 subset
d3.hok2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d3.hok2_meta.df=d3.hok2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 1 subset
d3.sek1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d3.sek1_meta.df=d3.sek1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 2 subset
d3.sek2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "3"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d3.sek2_meta.df=d3.sek2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#merge z scored dfs
d3.z_scored_df = rbind(d3.hok1_meta.df, d3.hok2_meta.df, d3.sek1_meta.df, d3.sek2_meta.df)

#anovas
aov_d3=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= d3.z_scored_df )

aov_d3_od=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing, data= d3.z_scored_df )

AIC(aov_d3, aov_d3_od)
summary(aov_d3_od)
plot(aov_d3_od)

d3.z_scored_df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  ggplot(aes(x= pairing, y =od_corrected_b_n ))+
    geom_boxplot()+
      geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") 


```



##### Day 5
```{r}
##### Day 1
#HOK 1 subset
d5.hok1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d5.hok1_meta.df=d5.hok1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))


#HOK 2 subset
d5.hok2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "HOK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d5.hok2_meta.df=d5.hok2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 1 subset
d5.sek1_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "1", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d5.sek1_meta.df=d5.sek1_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#SEK 2 subset
d5.sek2_meta.df <- data.frame(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$deploy == "SEK" &  meta.450.df$decomp_day == "5"& meta.450.df$hours == "24"& meta.450.df$site_num == "2", ])
# Calculate z-scores 
d5.sek2_meta.df=d5.sek2_meta.df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
  mutate(z_score= (od_corrected_b_n - mean(od_corrected_b_n))/sd(od_corrected_b_n))

#merge z scored dfs
d5.z_scored_df = rbind(d5.hok1_meta.df, d5.hok2_meta.df, d5.sek1_meta.df, d5.sek2_meta.df)

#anovas
aov_d5=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= d5.z_scored_df )

aov_d5_od=aov(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing, data= d5.z_scored_df )

lmer_d5_od=lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing +(1 | deploy)  + (1 | tree_id) + (1 | tree_num), data= d5.z_scored_df )
view(d5.z_scored_df)

AIC(aov_d5_od, lmer_d5_od)

summary(aov_d5_od)
plot(aov_d5_od)


# Plot boxplots with letters
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsignif)

# Ensure that the 'pairing' variable is a factor
d5.z_scored_df$pairing <- factor(d5.z_scored_df$pairing)

# Create the boxplot
ggplot(d5.z_scored_df, aes(x = pairing, y = od_corrected_b_n)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = mean(od_corrected_b_n), linetype = "Average"), 
             color = alpha("red", 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = tukey_result, method = "anova", label = "p.adj") +
  theme_bw()



```






```{r}
aov_d3=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="3" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(aov_d3)
plot(aov_d3)

aov_d5=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="5" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(aov_d5)
plot(aov_d5)

lmer_d5=aov(z_score ~ pairing + (1 | tree_num) + ( 1 | ), data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="5" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(lmer_d5)

AIC(aov_d5, lmer_d5)

aov_d7=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="7" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(aov_d7)
plot(aov_d7)

aov_d11=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="11" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(aov_d11)
plot(aov_d11)

aov_d17=aov(z_score ~ pairing, data= na.omit(z_scored_df[z_scored_df$decomp_day=="17" & z_scored_df$hours=="24",]) )
summary(aov_d17)

AIC(aov1, aov2)

plot(aov2)

TukeyHSD(aov2)
TukeyHSD(aov1)


z_scored_df%>%
  na.omit()%>%
ggplot(aes(x = pairing, y = z_score, color= pairing))+
    geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
    geom_hline(data = na.omit(z_scored_df), aes(yintercept = mean(z_score), linetype = "Average"), color = alpha("red",0.9), linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(.~decomp_day, scales = "free_y")+
  theme_classic()

  geom_point(aes(shape = pairing), position = position_jitter(width = 0.2)) 


```

Model exploration
**A note on GAMs**
The term "parametric" is often used to describe statistical models with a fixed, finite number of parameters. In contrast, "nonparametric" models are more flexible and can adapt to the complexity of the data without assuming a specific functional form.

Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) can be considered semiparametric. They are a type of regression model that extends generalized linear models (GLMs) by allowing for more flexible, nonlinear relationships between predictors and the response variable. The "additive" part refers to the fact that the model is built by adding together multiple smooth functions, each representing the relationship between a predictor and the response.

While GAMs introduce flexibility with the smooth functions, they still include parametric components, such as linear terms and parametric link functions. The parametric components provide a structured foundation, and the smooth functions capture additional complexity in the data.

So, we are using GAMsm that combine parametric and nonparametric elements to provide a flexible framework for modeling complex relationships in the metabolism assay data.

```{r}
lmer1 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 | deploy) + (1 | tree_id)+ (1|tree_num), subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

glmer1 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 | deploy) + (1 | tree_id)+ (1|tree_num), subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

AIC(lmer2, gam_trt.grp)
summary(lmer1)

plot(resid(lmer1))
qqnorm(resid(lmer1))
qqline(resid(lmer1))


lmer2 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 + treatment | decomp_day) + (1 | tree_num) + (1 | tree_id) + (1 | site_num) , subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

lmer3 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 + treatment /group | decomp_day) + (1 | tree_num) + (1 | tree_id) + (1 | site_num) , subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

summary(lmer3)


lmer4 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 + group /treatment | decomp_day) + (1 | tree_num) + (1 | tree_id) + (1 | site_num) , subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

AIC(lmer1, lmer2, lmer3, lmer4)



```


```{r}
lmer1 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="1" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer1)

lmer3 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="3" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer3)

lmer5 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="5" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer5)

lmer7 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="7" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer7)


lmer11 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="11" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer11)
plot(resid(lmer11))


lmer17 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="17" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
aov(lmer17)




lmer2 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + (1 | deploy) + (1|site_num) + (1 |tree_id) + (1|tree_num),  data = na.omit(meta.450.df[meta.450.df$decomp_day=="5" & meta.450.df$hours=="24",]))
summary(lmer2)

AIC(lmer1, glmer2)

lmer2 = lmer(z_score ~ treatment * group + (1 | decomp_day)  +  (1 | tree_id) + (1|tree_num), subset = hours == "24", data = na.omit(z_scored_df))

AIC(lmer1, lmer2)

lmer2 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + (1 | decomp_day) + (1 | deploy) +  (1 | tree_id) + (1|tree_num), subset = hours == "24", data = meta.450.df)

AIC(lmer1, lmer2)
lmer3 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( 1 + treatment + group | decomp_day)  + (1 | tree_id/tree_num) + (1|deploy/site_num), subset = hours == "24", data = meta.450.df)

lmer4 = lmer(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + ( treatment * group || decomp_day)  + (1 | tree_id/tree_num) , subset = hours == "24", data = meta.450.df)


gam1= gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day, pairing, bs = "fs", k = 5)  +s(tree_num, k = 12, bs = 're') + s(tree_id, bs = 're') + 
                   s(site_num, bs = 're') + s(deploy, bs = 're'),  method = "REML", 
                 data = meta.450.df)
summary(gam1)
gam.check(gam1)
#REML is generally preferred if you are primarily interested in estimating fixed effects and want better estimates of variance components. It's suitable for mixed-effects models.


#testing GAMs

gam1=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day ,k =6) + s(tree_id, tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy, site_num, bs="re"), subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam2=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day, by= pairing, k =6) + s(tree_id,bs="re") + s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"), subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam3=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k =6) + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s(tree_id,bs="re") + s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"), subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))
#####
gam4=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment + group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k =6) + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s(tree_id,bs="re") + s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"), subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))
########

gam5=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day, by= pairing, k=6)  + s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam6=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day, by= pairing, k=6)  + s(tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") +  s(site_num, deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))


gam7=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment + group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k=6)+s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam8=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment + group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k=6)+s(tree_id, tree_num, bs="re")+ s(deploy, site_num, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

#########

gam9=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k=6)+s(tree_num, bs="re") + s(deploy,bs="re") + s(site_num, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam10=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_id, tree_num, bs="re", k= 20) + s(deploy, site_num, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam11=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam12=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( site_num, tree_num, bs="re") + s(tree_id, bs="re") +  + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))



AIC( gam10, gam11, gam12)

####### 2 canditate models
####### first lets look at main effects and interaction of treatment and group

# looks at interaction between main effects
tw_gam_trt.grp=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

tw_gam_trt.grp2=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment + group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re")  + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

tw_gam_trt.grp3=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

tw_gam_trt.grp4=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment*group + s(decomp_day, treatment, bs="fs", k =6)+ s(decomp_day,group,bs="fs", k =6)+ s(tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num, deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

summary(tw_gam_trt.grp4)

AIC(tw_gam_trt.grp, tw_gam_trt.grp2, tw_gam_trt.grp3,tw_gam_trt.grp4 )

concurvity(tw_gam_trt.grp4)

inv_gam_trt.grp=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = gaussian(link = "inverse"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

log_gam_trt.grp=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = gaussian(link = "log"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

gam_trt.grp=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ treatment * group + s(decomp_day, by= treatment, k=6)  + s(decomp_day, by= group, k =6)+ s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = gaussian(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))

AIC(tw_gam_trt.grp, gam_trt.grp)

summary(tw_gam_trt.grp)
gam.check(tw_gam_trt.grp)
anova.gam(gam_trt.grp)

#plot difference for P vs AB
plot_difference(
  gam_trt.grp,
  series = decomp_day,
  difference = list(treatment = c("P", "AB"))
) + 
  Fig.formatting 
  #this is important to see where the differences in the smooths lie given that the term is significant (p = 0.002)


#plot difference for P vs AB
plot_difference(
  gam_trt.grp,
  series = decomp_day,
  difference = list(group = c("H", "AW"))
) + 
  Fig.formatting 

####### next we can look at paired main effects for pairwise comparison
#looks at individual treatments
gam_pair=gam(od_corrected_b_n ~ pairing + s(decomp_day, by= pairing, k=6) + s( tree_num, tree_id, bs="re") + s(site_num,bs="re") + s(deploy, bs="re"),  subset = hours == "24",family = tw(link = "identity"), method = "REML", data = na.omit(meta.450.df))
summary(gam_pair)
gam.check(gam_pair)

AIC(gam_trt.grp, gam_pair, lmer1)
BIC(gam_trt.grp, gam_pair)
anova.gam(gam_pair)

#### in our model (gam_pair), the reference level is set to P-AW, so we can inspect the differences between smooths relative to P-AW

#plot difference for PAW and PH
```
